decisions.title: Prioritize Chat/Agent UX Polish vs Core Explore V2 Stability
decisions.context: Heavy engineering investment in chat UI (markdown, lexical, shiki syntax highlighting, thinking events) while core data infrastructure issues remain (S3 throttling, PyRunner prod outage, duplicate data points). Need to decide if we're 'Nailing the Core' or prematurely moving to new surfaces.

arguments.for: Chat/Agent represents 'Embed Deeper' pillar and new decision capture surface. Early UX investment creates competitive moat. Parallel teams can work both tracks.
arguments.against: Core Explore V2 has production stability issues (PyRunner outage, S3 throttling, duplicate data). Strategy says 'Nail the Core' first. Customers can't make decisions if core platform is unreliable. Focus fragmentation risks both initiatives.

decisions.title: Commit to Workspace-Level Data Alignment as V2 Differentiator
decisions.context: Design work on workspace-level alignment (DES-79) and annotation-based time setting (DES-75, ENG-10393) suggests major UX shift. This could be Canvas differentiator but requires significant engineering lift while team is split across chat, data infra, and core features.

arguments.for: Workspace-level alignment solves real user pain around multi-source correlation. Aligns with 'Decisions Captured' north star by making cross-dataset decisions easier. Could be Canvas killer feature.
arguments.against: Large scope addition while core features (live mode with relative time, annotation publishing) still incomplete. Risk diluting Explore V2 launch impact. May be premature optimization before validating simpler alignment flows.

decisions.title: Accelerate Python Rules Production Readiness or Pause Customer Rollout
decisions.context: PyRunner pod availability issues in prod (ENG-9056) while continuing to build frontend features. If Python Rules is a 2026 pillar ('Build the Moat'), production stability is existential. Need go/no-go decision.
decisions.blockedBy: Root cause analysis of PyRunner prod failures incomplete. No clear remediation timeline.

arguments.for: Pause new customer rollouts until stability proven. Protects brand and prevents churn. Allows focused sprint on infrastructure before expanding surface area.
arguments.against: Slows revenue pipeline if Python Rules is competitive differentiator. May lose early adopter feedback window. Problem could be isolated/resolvable quickly.

decisions.title: Sunset Channel IDs in Data Architecture This Quarter
decisions.context: Three parallel efforts to remove channel ID dependencies (ENG-10508, 10507, 10509) suggest architectural debt cleanup. This is 'Nail the Core' work but requires coordination across influx-ingestion, Flink jobs, and backend.

arguments.for: Channel ID removal unblocks multi-tenancy improvements and simplifies data model. Foundation for scale. All three issues in flight suggests team has momentum.
arguments.against: Risk of data pipeline breakage during migration. May not be customer-visible improvement. Could defer to focus on user-facing features that drive North Star metric.

decisions.title: Define Chat Service API Stability Contract Before Broader Integration
decisions.context: Multiple chat service changes (enums replacing strings, UID returns, thinking events, message formats) while simultaneously building E2E tests and frontend integrations. Need API freeze point or continuous breakage risk.

arguments.for: Early in development cycle - iterate fast, break things, optimize for learning. E2E tests (ENG-10375) will catch regressions. Premature API freeze slows innovation.
arguments.against: Frontend team building on shifting foundation (markdown, lexical, thinking events all in parallel). Each breaking change cascades. Define v1 contract, freeze, then iterate to v2.

northStar.name: Decisions Captured Per Customer
northStar.value: Unable to measure - no instrumentation signals

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy engineering execution week - 30 issues in flight across 4 major initiatives (chat, explore, data infra, python rules). Low coordination overhead visible (no all-hands or planning meetings in signals). Risk: fragmentation without strategic alignment check-ins.
alerts: No signals of customer feedback loops - building in vacuum?
PyRunner prod outage (ENG-9056) has no visible incident review or postmortem signal
Zero Product/Design partnership signals on chat features - eng-driven UX risk
S3 throttling and data quality issues suggest scaling problems not staffed adequately
